title: Making ‘Midsommar,’ A Deep Dive Into the Movie Freakout of the Year | Interview
format: Sean and Chris talk through Ari Aster's Midsommar, then Sean sits down with Aster on building the Hårga village, casting Florence Pugh, and the eleven scripts in his drawer.

segments.heading: Midsommar
segments.note: Sean and Chris, spoilers kept to a minimum

films.title: Midsommar
films.note: Aster's daytime folk-horror breakup movie; a group of grad students follow a friend to his Swedish commune. Both hosts came out blown away and a little rattled.

films.title: Hereditary
films.note: The 2018 debut they keep measuring Midsommar against; more supernatural, more two-movies-in-one, where this one plays as a single cohesive sentence.

films.title: Lady Macbeth
films.note: Where Sean first clocked Florence Pugh, whom he calls one of the best performances of the year.

films.title: Little Women
films.note: Greta Gerwig's adaptation, out later in 2019, also starring Pugh; Sean jokes it was its own kind of horror movie for a certain generation of men.

films.title: Midsommar
films.note: Aster frames it as a macabre fairy tale and an operatic breakup movie; the Hårga village was built from scratch in a Hungarian field, scored by Bobby Krlic as the Haxan Cloak.

films.title: Hereditary
films.note: He was in Hungarian pre-production the day after it opened, so he never really felt its reception.

films.title: Lady Macbeth
films.note: The William Oldroyd film that sold him on Pugh, who couldn't audition at first because she was shooting Park Chan-wook's Little Drummer Girl.

films.title: Fighting With My Family
films.note: Cited as part of Pugh's run of demanding roles, for which she had to learn to wrestle.

films.title: The Wicker Man
films.note: The brightly lit pagan-horror touchstone Aster reaches for, though he notes it's mostly grim and overcast.

films.title: The Color of Pomegranates
films.note: Sergei Parajanov film Aster had production designer Henrik Svensson watch.

films.title: Shadows of Forgotten Ancestors
films.note: The other Parajanov reference for the film's design.

films.title: The Wizard of Oz
films.note: A color reference, especially the garish 'Crayola phase' near the end; they also studied 40s and 50s MGM musicals.

films.title: Last Year at Marienbad
films.note: A restored Alain Resnais DCP he caught at Film Forum. He calls it a recent favorite, and traces Roeg's montage back to it.

films.title: Don't Look Now
films.note: The Nicolas Roeg film he says is deeply important to him, talked about while touring Hereditary.

films.title: The Souvenir
films.note: The Joanna Hogg film that recently made him jealous.

films.title: Exhibition
films.note: An earlier Hogg film he loves; he's been a fan a long time.
